items.title: Chrome V8 Zero-Day CVE-2026-11645 Exploited in the Wild — Patch Now
items.summary: Google patched an actively exploited out-of-bounds read/write flaw in Chrome's V8 JavaScript engine (CVE-2026-11645). The bug enables remote code execution inside the Chrome sandbox via a crafted HTML page. Patch shipped June 8, 2026 in Chrome 149.0.7827.102/.103.
items.why: Affects every desktop running Chrome; sandbox RCE via a malicious webpage with confirmed in-the-wild exploitation makes this a top-priority patch for all orgs. Update immediately.

items.title: Critical Check Point VPN Auth Bypass CVE-2026-50751 (CVSS 9.3) Exploited by Qilin Ransomware
items.summary: A critical IKEv1 authentication bypass in Check Point Remote Access VPN lets unauthenticated attackers establish VPN sessions without credentials. Active exploitation confirmed since May 7, 2026; CISA added it to KEV on June 8 with a 3-day federal patch deadline. A Qilin ransomware affiliate has been linked to attacks using this flaw.
items.why: CVSS 9.3 authentication bypass in widely-deployed enterprise VPN with ransomware actors already inside victim networks. CISA KEV listing means immediate remediation is required for federal agencies and strongly recommended for all.

items.title: APT28 Operation Neusploit: Russian GRU Targets European Maritime & Defense Orgs via MS Office Zero-Day
items.summary: Russian GRU-linked APT28 (Fancy Bear) ran a concentrated 72-hour spear-phishing campaign (Jan 28-30, 2026) exploiting CVE-2026-21509, a Microsoft Office security-feature bypass that triggers without macros. Targets included defense ministries and transport agencies in Poland, Slovenia, Turkey, Greece, UAE, and Ukraine. The infection chain drops a novel Outlook VBA backdoor (NotDoor) and a modified Covenant C2 implant that beacons to filen.io cloud storage.
items.why: Nation-state campaign combining a stealthy MS Office zero-day with legitimate cloud C2 infrastructure to blend into normal traffic — a blueprint other APTs will replicate against NATO-adjacent critical infrastructure.

items.title: Iranian APT 'Screening Serpens' Surges Post-Feb 2026, Deploys New MiniUpdate & MiniJunk V2 RATs
items.summary: Palo Alto Unit 42 tracks Iranian state-linked group Screening Serpens, which sharply escalated operations after February 2026, deploying two new RAT families across targets in at least five countries: MiniUpdate (a newly discovered family) and MiniJunk V2 (an evolved iteration). Infection chains begin with targeted spear-phishing and use DLL sideloading for execution.
items.why: New malware families from a nation-state actor with a documented operational surge tied to a specific geopolitical trigger — signals Iranian cyber posture has shifted toward broader espionage collection.

items.title: CrowdStrike 2026 Global Threat Report: eCrime Breakout Time Collapses to 29 Min, AI Adversaries Up 89%
items.summary: CrowdStrike's 2026 annual report reveals the average eCrime breakout time — the window defenders have before lateral movement begins — dropped to 29 minutes (fastest ever recorded: 27 seconds). AI-enabled adversary activity rose 89% YoY. Russia-nexus FANCY BEAR deployed LAMEHUG, an LLM-powered malware that automates reconnaissance and document exfiltration. Adversaries also injected malicious prompts into GenAI tools at 90+ organizations to steal credentials and crypto.
items.why: A 29-minute breakout window means traditional IR timelines are obsolete. The LAMEHUG LLM-in-the-loop malware sets a new precedent for AI-assisted post-compromise automation that defenders must account for in detection engineering.
